description_long: This object consists of a group of industrial metal pipes and barrels, arranged upright and at angles, resembling plumbing or ductwork components commonly found in hardware stores. The pipes are cylindrical, with visible seams and flanges at the joints, and come in varying heights and diameters. The surfaces are painted in muted shades of green and brown, with some areas showing signs of wear, grime, or oxidation, giving them a used, utilitarian appearance. The barrels are similarly colored and have a ribbed texture, suggesting they are made of metal. The overall look is rugged and functional, suitable for use in construction, plumbing, or industrial settings. The objects are free-standing and do not appear to be connected to any larger structure.
description: A group of upright and angled metal pipes and barrels in green and brown, with visible seams and a worn, industrial finish.
description_view_0: Three upright barrels and two angled pipes are visible, showing the ribbed texture and color variation between green and brown.
description_view_1: The largest angled pipe is prominent in the foreground, with the tops of the barrels and pipes visible behind it.
description_view_2: The angled pipe and barrel groupings are seen from the side, highlighting the different heights and the metallic surface details.
description_view_3: The tops of the pipes and barrels are visible, emphasizing their cylindrical shapes and the arrangement of the group.
attribute_storefront_layout: The exterior consists of a loose grouping of upright and angled pipes and barrels, with no visible entrances, windows, or display areas.
